report_metadata.cancer_type: Nasal Cavity Squamous Cell Carcinoma with Ethmoid Extension — Occupational (Nickel)
report_metadata.patient_context: 45-year-old male nickel refinery worker with occupational nasal cavity SCC, T3 with ethmoid extension, distinct from maxillary sinus SCC

treatments.name: Endoscopic Medial Maxillectomy + Ethmoidectomy + Adjuvant Radiation Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective studies and pooled analyses (1,404 cases) demonstrating equivalent or superior outcomes to open approaches; NCCN-recommended endoscopic approach for appropriate sinonasal cancers

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 78% with negative margins in definitive resection group; 5-year OS 93% with negative margins specifically; local recurrence 17.8% endoscopic vs 38.5% open; nasal cavity primary has better prognosis (77% 5-year survival) than maxillary sinus (62%)

accessibility.rationale: Requires experienced endoscopic skull base surgeon; available at major academic centers and tertiary ENT departments; specialized equipment needed

safety_profile.rationale: Lower morbidity than open craniofacial resection; no facial incision; shorter hospital stay; faster healing; preserves facial cosmesis; avoids craniotomy in most T3 cases

biomarker_match.rationale: Ideal for nasal cavity primary with ethmoid extension (T3N0); endoscopic approach well-suited for laterally based tumors requiring medial maxillectomy; occupational etiology does not change surgical approach

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Transnasal endoscopic resection of the lateral nasal wall (medial maxillectomy) with complete ethmoidectomy for tumor extending into ethmoid sinuses. Boundaries include nasal floor inferiorly, cribriform plate/fovea ethmoidalis superiorly, anterior maxillary wall anteriorly, and posterior ethmoid posteriorly. Frozen section margin analysis during surgery. Adjuvant RT (60-66 Gy) to tumor bed addresses microscopic residual disease risk.

key_evidence.study_name: Endoscopic surgery for SCC in nasal cavity and ethmoid sinus: retrospective observational study

notable_side_effects: Rare CSF leak if approaching skull base

treatments.name: Open Craniofacial Resection + Adjuvant Radiation Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Gold standard with decades of evidence; 25-year institutional experience series (craniofacial resection); historical standard against which endoscopic approaches are compared

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 51% in large series; higher morbidity but potentially wider margins for T3 tumors with extensive ethmoid involvement or skull base proximity; salvage potential if endoscopic resection fails

accessibility.rationale: Available at head and neck surgery centers; well-established technique; neurosurgical collaboration for craniotomy component

safety_profile.rationale: Major surgery with craniotomy; facial incision (lateral rhinotomy or Weber-Ferguson); higher complication rates than endoscopic; frontal lobe retraction; CSF leak risk; longer recovery; wound complications

biomarker_match.rationale: Indicated for T3-T4 tumors with significant skull base involvement or when endoscopic margins are not achievable; may be needed for extensive ethmoid disease approaching cribriform plate

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combined transfacial and transcranial approach to achieve en bloc resection of nasal cavity and ethmoid tumors with adequate margins. Includes lateral rhinotomy or midfacial degloving for access, with frontal craniotomy if cribriform plate or dura involved. Allows wide-field resection and direct visualization of skull base margin.

key_evidence.study_name: Craniofacial resection for tumors of the nasal cavity and paranasal sinuses: 25-year experience

biomarker_requirements: Skull base involvement requiring open approach
treatments.availability: Widely Available at H&N Surgery Centers

treatments.name: Adjuvant Proton Beam Therapy (IMPT) — Post-Surgical

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple institutional studies including 54-patient series for sinonasal SCC; NCCN supports proton therapy for this disease site; meta-analysis showing reduced local recurrence vs photon therapy

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year and 5-year local control 80%; 5-year OS 47% for advanced sinonasal SCC; particle therapy reduces local recurrence vs photon therapy; spot scanning proton therapy achieves 92% 2-year local control

accessibility.rationale: Limited proton therapy centers; expensive; insurance coverage variable but NCCN supports use in sinonasal malignancies; growing availability

safety_profile.rationale: Superior sparing of optic nerves, brain, and contralateral structures vs photon IMRT; reduced risk of radiation-induced optic neuropathy and brain necrosis; lower mean doses to critical structures

biomarker_match.rationale: Ideal for nasal cavity/ethmoid tumors adjacent to critical structures (eyes, brain, optic chiasm); dosimetric advantage greatest for this anatomic location

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Intensity-modulated proton therapy (IMPT) using pencil beam scanning delivers conformal dose to the post-operative tumor bed while exploiting the Bragg peak to minimize dose to adjacent critical structures — optic nerves, optic chiasm, brain, contralateral eye, and lacrimal glands. Particularly advantageous for sinonasal tumors where the target is surrounded by dose-limiting structures.

key_evidence.study_name: Long-Term Outcomes After Proton Beam Therapy for Sinonasal SCC
key_evidence.journal: International Journal of Radiation Oncology, Biology, Physics

treatments.name: Surgery + Adjuvant Cisplatin-Based Chemoradiation (High-Risk Features)

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN-recommended for high-risk features (positive margins, perineural invasion, lymphovascular invasion); extrapolated from HNSCC adjuvant CRT data; institutional series for sinonasal SCC

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year local control 81%, regional control 90%, OS 80%; concurrent cisplatin improves locoregional control in high-risk resected disease; justified if positive margins or perineural invasion found

accessibility.rationale: Cisplatin + RT widely available at all oncology centers; standard chemotherapy regimen; well-established protocols

safety_profile.rationale: Cisplatin nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, peripheral neuropathy on top of RT side effects; mucositis; significant acute toxicity; 6-week treatment course

biomarker_match.rationale: Reserved for high-risk pathologic features; T3 with ethmoid extension may have close margins or perineural invasion warranting concurrent chemo

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Concurrent cisplatin (100 mg/m2 on days 1, 22, 43) with adjuvant radiation therapy (60-66 Gy). Cisplatin radiosensitizes tumor cells through platinum-DNA crosslinks, enhancing radiation-induced cell death. Indicated when surgical pathology reveals high-risk features such as positive margins, perineural spread, perivascular invasion, or extensive ethmoid involvement.

key_evidence.study_name: Definitive and adjuvant radiotherapy for sinonasal SCC: single institutional experience

treatments.name: Definitive Radiation Therapy Alone (Non-Surgical Candidate)

evidence_level.rationale: Established treatment option for nasal cavity SCC; NCI states surgery and RT have equal cure rates for nasal cavity tumors; institutional series available

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year survival 77% for nasal cavity primary; equal cure rates to surgery per NCI guidelines; however surgery + adjuvant RT preferred for T3 disease in most guidelines

accessibility.rationale: Universally available at radiation oncology centers; avoids surgical morbidity; non-invasive approach

safety_profile.rationale: Avoids surgical complications; however radiation to sinonasal region risks optic neuropathy, brain necrosis, xerostomia, chronic sinusitis, nasal dryness

biomarker_match.rationale: Applicable to all nasal cavity SCC; primarily reserved for unresectable tumors or patients declining surgery

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Definitive external beam radiation therapy (typically 66-70 Gy in 33-35 fractions) using IMRT technique targeting the primary tumor in the nasal cavity and ethmoid sinuses with margins. Aims for complete tumor eradication without surgery.

key_evidence.study_name: NCI Paranasal Sinus and Nasal Cavity Cancer Treatment PDQ

treatments.name: Carbon Ion Radiation Therapy (CIRT)

evidence_level.rationale: Large retrospective series (2,282 cases from real world); institutional experience; no Phase 3 RCTs vs proton or photon therapy; promising outcome data

survival_benefit.rationale: 3-year OS 75% and 3-year local control 79.6% for treatment-naive patients; higher RBE than protons potentially more effective for radioresistant tumors

accessibility.rationale: Very limited global availability — Japan, Germany, China, Italy; extremely expensive; not available in most countries; requires specialized facility

safety_profile.rationale: No acute grade 3+ toxicities in some series; late grade 3+ in 3 patients; Bragg peak advantage; superior dose conformality; higher RBE may cause more late effects in some cases

biomarker_match.rationale: Carbon ions have higher RBE than protons, potentially advantageous for radioresistant SCC; particularly beneficial for sinonasal tumors adjacent to critical structures

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carbon ion therapy delivers highly conformal radiation with both Bragg peak dose distribution and higher relative biological effectiveness (RBE ~3x photons) compared to protons. The higher linear energy transfer (LET) causes more complex, irreparable DNA damage. Particularly advantageous for radioresistant tumors in anatomically complex locations like the sinonasal region.

key_evidence.study_name: Carbon ion radiation therapy for sinonasal malignancies: results from 2,282 real-world cases

treatments.availability: Very Limited — Japan, Germany, China, Italy

treatments.name: Induction Chemotherapy (TPF) Followed by Surgery or Definitive CRT

evidence_level.rationale: Comprehensive review of induction chemotherapy for locoregionally advanced SNSCC; Phase 2 data; not standard first-line but used for advanced/borderline resectable cases

survival_benefit.rationale: Response rates to induction TPF allow assessment of tumor chemosensitivity; responders may achieve organ preservation or improved surgical outcomes; data limited for nasal cavity SCC specifically

accessibility.rationale: TPF widely available; standard chemotherapy regimen; requires close monitoring during induction

safety_profile.rationale: TPF has significant toxicity — febrile neutropenia, mucositis, nephrotoxicity; delays definitive surgery; risk of disease progression during induction

biomarker_match.rationale: T3 with ethmoid extension may benefit from tumor downsizing before surgery; however T3N0 nasal cavity is typically resectable upfront

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Docetaxel/cisplatin/5-fluorouracil (TPF) induction chemotherapy given for 2-3 cycles before definitive treatment. Aims to reduce tumor volume, test chemosensitivity, and potentially improve resectability. Responders proceed to less extensive surgery or definitive CRT; non-responders proceed directly to surgery.

key_evidence.study_name: Induction Chemotherapy for Locoregionally Advanced SNSCC: Comprehensive Review

treatments.name: Perioperative Pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-689 Extrapolation)

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-689 included laryngeal/hypopharyngeal/oral cavity HNSCC; sinonasal site not specifically included but extrapolation reasonable for SCC histology; Phase 2 data for SNSCC with pembrolizumab

survival_benefit.rationale: KEYNOTE-689 showed EFS improvement in LA HNSCC (57.6% vs 46.4% at 3 years); Phase 2 SNSCC data showed ORR 60% with pembrolizumab + chemotherapy; applicability to nasal cavity SCC uncertain

accessibility.rationale: Pembrolizumab FDA-approved; perioperative use for HNSCC expanding; PD-L1 testing standard

safety_profile.rationale: Immune-related AEs added to surgical and RT toxicity; grade 3+ TRAEs ~44%; manageable in most patients

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 status not specified for this patient; SCC histology is checkpoint-responsive; sinonasal SCC immunotherapy data still limited

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab (2 cycles before surgery) activates anti-tumor immunity, potentially improving resection margins and pathologic response. Adjuvant pembrolizumab post-surgery maintains immune surveillance against micrometastatic disease. Extrapolated from KEYNOTE-689 HNSCC data to sinonasal SCC.

treatments.name: Definitive Concurrent Chemoradiation (Unresectable T3)

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN-recommended for unresectable sinonasal malignancies; institutional series; standard concurrent cisplatin + RT protocol

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year OS 80% in institutional series; inferior to surgery + adjuvant RT for resectable T3; reserved for truly unresectable disease or patient refusal of surgery

accessibility.rationale: Available at all oncology centers; standard chemoradiation protocol

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity — mucositis, nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity; radiation to orbit and brain at full dose; 6-7 week treatment course

biomarker_match.rationale: Non-specific; applicable to all unresectable sinonasal SCC regardless of biomarkers

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Concurrent cisplatin (100 mg/m2 days 1, 22, 43 or weekly 40 mg/m2) with definitive radiation therapy (70 Gy in 35 fractions). Cisplatin radiosensitization combined with full-dose RT aims for complete tumor eradication without surgery.

key_evidence.study_name: Outcomes of nonsurgical management of locally advanced sinonasal carcinomas

treatments.name: Elective Neck Irradiation (Controversial for N0 Nasal Cavity SCC)

evidence_level.rationale: No consensus on elective neck treatment for N0 nasal cavity SCC; low rate of occult nodal metastasis (~10-15%); NCCN recommends consideration but not mandatory

survival_benefit.rationale: Occult nodal rate low for nasal cavity primary (lower than maxillary sinus); prophylactic neck irradiation may prevent regional failure in subset; but most N0 patients are observed

accessibility.rationale: Standard radiation technique; can be incorporated into adjuvant RT treatment plan

safety_profile.rationale: Additional radiation field increases toxicity — xerostomia, dysphagia, hypothyroidism; treatment-related morbidity for a low-probability event

biomarker_match.rationale: T3 stage and SCC histology have higher nodal risk than T1-2; but N0 at presentation suggests adequate staging; clinical judgment required

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Prophylactic radiation to bilateral levels IB-III neck lymph nodes to address potential occult micrometastatic disease. For N0 nasal cavity SCC, the risk of occult nodal disease is approximately 10-15%, making elective neck treatment controversial. Decision based on T-stage, histologic grade, and margin status.

key_evidence.study_name: Development and validation of prognostic model for nasal cavity SCC — SEER database

biomarker_requirements: Risk stratification for occult nodal disease

clinical_trials.title: Cisplatin/Etoposide + Adjuvant Proton Radiotherapy in Nasal Cavity and Paranasal Sinus Cancer
biomarker_requirements: Nasal cavity or paranasal sinus carcinoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating combined chemotherapy with proton therapy for sinonasal malignancies; dose-response and toxicity assessment ongoing

clinical_trials.title: Perioperative Pembrolizumab in Resectable LA HNSCC
biomarker_requirements: Resectable Stage III-IVA HNSCC (sinonasal subsite eligible)
clinical_trials.early_results: 36-month EFS 57.6% vs 46.4%; practice-changing for perioperative immunotherapy in resectable HNSCC; extrapolatable to sinonasal SCC

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab + Chemotherapy for Recurrent/Metastatic Sinonasal SCC
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 60% and DCR 100% with pembrolizumab + nab-paclitaxel + cisplatin in 20 patients

clinical_trials.title: Intensity-Modulated Proton Therapy (IMPT) for Sinonasal Malignancies
biomarker_requirements: Sinonasal malignancy requiring adjuvant or definitive RT
clinical_trials.early_results: 2-year local control 92% with spot scanning proton therapy; superior dosimetry to photon IMRT

clinical_trials.title: Carbon Ion Radiation Therapy for Locally Advanced Sinonasal Malignancies
clinical_trials.early_results: 3-year OS 75%, 3-year local control 79.6% for treatment-naive patients; promising for radioresistant histologies

combination_strategies.rationale: Combining minimally invasive endoscopic surgery with proton therapy provides both excellent oncologic outcomes and superior normal tissue sparing. Proton therapy reduces risk of optic neuropathy, brain necrosis, and contralateral eye toxicity compared to photon IMRT — critical advantage for ethmoid tumors adjacent to orbits and skull base.

Addition of concurrent cisplatin to adjuvant RT for high-risk features (positive margins, PNI, LVI) improves locoregional control. T3 with ethmoid extension may harbor high-risk pathologic features warranting chemoradiation.

Adding perioperative pembrolizumab to surgical + adjuvant standard of care may improve EFS. SCC histology is checkpoint-responsive. Rational extrapolation from KEYNOTE-689 to sinonasal SCC pending formal sinonasal-specific data.

Combining particle therapy's dosimetric advantage with cisplatin radiosensitization aims for maximum tumor control while preserving adjacent critical structures. Active clinical trial evaluating this approach.

supportive_care.approach: Occupational Health Assessment and Workers' Compensation
supportive_care.evidence: IARC classification of nickel compounds as Group 1 carcinogens; SIR 18.0 for nasal cancer in nickel refinery workers; sentinel cancer for occupational exposure
supportive_care.benefit: Legal recognition of occupational origin enables workers' compensation coverage for treatment costs; occupational health documentation critical; nickel refinery SCC classified as occupational disease in most jurisdictions; removal from continued nickel exposure mandatory

supportive_care.approach: Nasal Irrigation and Sinonasal Hygiene Post-Surgery
supportive_care.evidence: Post-operative sinonasal care guidelines; endoscopic surgery recovery protocols
supportive_care.benefit: Regular saline irrigation reduces crusting, promotes mucosal healing, maintains nasal airway patency, and enables early detection of recurrence during office endoscopy surveillance

supportive_care.evidence: Radiation optic neuropathy literature; proton therapy ocular sparing studies
supportive_care.benefit: Serial visual acuity, visual field, and fundoscopic examination essential when RT delivered near optic nerves; early detection of radiation-induced optic neuropathy allows intervention; particularly important with ethmoid tumors adjacent to orbit

supportive_care.evidence: Nickel exposure + smoking synergistic cancer risk; smoking cessation improves treatment outcomes
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces risk of second primary cancer; improves wound healing; reduces radiation toxicity; nickel + smoking interaction multiplicatively increases nasal cancer risk

supportive_care.approach: Dental Assessment and Oral Care Pre-Radiation
supportive_care.evidence: Pre-RT dental evaluation guidelines; osteoradionecrosis prevention protocols
supportive_care.benefit: Pre-RT dental extraction of at-risk teeth prevents osteoradionecrosis; fluoride trays if salivary glands in field; oral hygiene optimization reduces RT-related oral complications

supportive_care.approach: Psychological Support and Facial Appearance Counseling
supportive_care.evidence: Head and neck cancer QoL literature; facial disfigurement psychological impact studies
supportive_care.benefit: Nasal cavity surgery may alter nasal appearance or function; psychological preparation and support for potential cosmetic changes; prosthetic rehabilitation if needed
